channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: New MH370 project searches for answers on vanished plane
item.description: A new project is searching for answers about what happened to Malaysian Airlines Flight MH370 and its 239 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Biden: 'I don't know' if Netanyahu trying to influence election
item.description: President Biden on Friday said he wasn't aware of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was trying to influence the U.S. election, while noting no administration has been a stronger ally to Israel than his own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: US launches airstrikes by fighter jets and ships on Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military struck more than a dozen Houthi targets in Yemen on Friday, going after weapons systems, bases and other equipment belonging to the Iranian-backed rebels, U.S. officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentine president delivered modified 'West Wing' monologue at UN General Assembly
item.description: Argentine President Javier Milei delivered a United Nations General Assembly address last month with remarkable similarities to a monologue recited by fictional U.S. President Jed Bartlet from “The West Wing." Toward the end of Milei’s Sept.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Israeli airstrikes rock southern suburbs of Beirut and cut off a key crossing into Syria
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel carried out another series of punishing airstrikes Friday, hitting suburban Beirut and cutting off the main border crossing between Lebanon and Syria for tens of thousands of people fleeing the Israeli bombardment of the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rainstorms and heavy floods hit large parts of Bosnia, killing at least 16 people
item.description: KISELJAK, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — A severe rainstorm struck Bosnia overnight Friday, killing at least 16 people in floods and landslides in several towns and villages in central and southern parts of the country, with surging waters rushing into people's homes as they were sleeping.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli military kills 250 Hezbollah terrorists since start of limited ground operation in Lebanon
item.description: Israel's military says it has killed 250 Hezbollah terrorists and has struck more than 2,000 military targets since it launched a limited ground operation in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US arranges flights to bring Americans out of Lebanon as others seek escape
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S.-arranged flights have brought about 350 Americans and their immediate relatives out of Lebanon this week during escalated fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, while thousands of others still there face airstrikes and diminishing commercial flights.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sanxingdui Ruins were discovered accidentally by a farmer; has since been the site of over 60K relics
item.description: Sanxingdui Ruins is an archaeological site in Guanghan City. Official archaeological excavations have been conducted since 1934 at the site after its discovery in 1929.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh in on Mexico's lawsuit against US gun manufacturers
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed Friday to take up the gun industry’s appeal of a ruling allowing the Mexican government to proceed with its lawsuit against several prominent American firearm manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Two London police officers reinstated over stop and search of Black athletes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Two former London police officers have been handed their jobs back and will receive back pay after winning an appeal Friday against a ruling that said they had lied about smelling cannabis during a stop and search of two Black athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Susan Rice: 'Surrender monkey' Trump is GOP's Neville Chamberlain
item.description: Former White House adviser Susan Rice criticized former President Trump over his foreign policy agenda, calling him an "appeaser" and likening him to former British Prime Minister Neville Chamberlain — who tried to appease Nazi leader Adolf Hitler.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Clashes in Kenya as people discuss the deputy president's impeachment motion
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Supporters and opponents of Kenya's deputy president clashed Friday at public forums over an impeachment motion against him, which was introduced in parliament by the ruling alliance this week.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: Relatives say an Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children.
item.dc:creator: Nasser Nasser And Aref Tufaha, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wildlife lover facing charges after allegedly assaulting passersby while attempting to capture a baby seagull
item.description: A Belfast woman was in court Tuesday after allegedly attacking passersby who tried to intervene with her capture of a baby seagull in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Nobel Prizes will be announced against a backdrop of wars, famine and artificial intelligence
item.description: Wars, a refugee crisis, famine, and artificial intelligence could all be recognized with Nobel Prizes.
item.dc:creator: Mark Lewis, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Japan's new leader expresses regret for governing party slush fund scandal, vows stronger defense
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba opened his first policy speech in office Friday by expressing deep regrets over the governing party's slush funds scandal that dogged his predecessor.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un threatened to use nuclear weapons and destroy South Korea permanently if provoked, state media reported Friday, after the South’s leader warned that Kim’s regime would collapse if he attempted to use nuclear arms.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bus filled with wedding guests plunges into a ravine, killing 7
item.description: A speeding bus filled with wedding guests crashed in southeastern Pakistan on Thursday, killing seven of the passengers while injuring 17 others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: G20 environment ministers back funding for forest conservation
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Environment ministers of the Group of 20 nations agreed Thursday to support the creation of funding sources for ecosystem services, acknowledging Brazil's proposal to establish a trust fund for forest conservation.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know as Israel expands its operations in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli airstrikes hammered targets across southern Lebanon and Beirut's suburbs while its military urged residents to flee more than two dozen Lebanese towns and villages, further signs that Israel is ramping up its operations in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 78 people died when a boat with 278 on board capsized in eastern Congo, official says
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — At least 78 people died when an overcrowded boat capsized on Lake Kivu in eastern Congo on Thursday, a local governor said. A frantic search and rescue operation was underway hours later as many remained unaccounted for from the vessel, believed to have had 278 people on board.
item.dc:creator: RUTH ALONGA, JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Africa's top court will decide if impeachment proceedings against the president can be revived
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South Africa's Constitutional Court said Thursday that it would hear a case next month brought by two opposition parties to revive impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over a scandal involving more than $500,000 in cash that was hidden in a couch a
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of migrants still missing off Djibouti's coast after smugglers forced them out of boats
item.description: MOGADISHU, Somalia (AP) — Rescuers searched Thursday for dozens of migrants from Africa still missing after smugglers apparently forced them out of two boats in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti earlier this week, the Djiboutian coast guard said.
item.dc:creator: OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Harpole Treasure includes one of the most valuable pieces of ancient jewelry found in Britain
item.description: The Harpole Treasure was discovered in 2022, and refers to a collection of valuable relics unearthed from a burial ground in England. Among the finds was a unique piece of jewelry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yazidi woman held hostage for 10 years in Gaza rescued in Israel, US operation
item.description: A Yazidi woman held captive by a Hamas terrorist after she was abducted by ISIS a decade ago has been returned to her family after she escaped through coordinated efforts by Israeli and U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Typhoon Krathon makes landfall in Taiwan, packing fierce winds and torrential rain
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Typhoon Krathon made landfall Thursday in Taiwan's major port city of Kaohsiung, bringing torrential rains and fierce winds to the island's south. Trees were brought down by high winds and roads were flooded, prompting the closure of schools and businesses.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This German church is the tallest in the world. Until Spain's La Sagrada Familia is done, anyway
item.description: ULM, Germany (AP) — The Ulmer Münster in southern Germany is the world's tallest church. For now, anyway. The Gothic-style Lutheran church's reign — begun in May 31, 1890 — could end in 2025, when La Sagrada Familia Basilica's “Tower of Jesus Christ” in Spain is set to be completed.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World War II-era bomb explodes at busy international airport, cratering taxiway
item.description: An American bomb from World War II buried beneath a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, cratering a taxiway and forcing the cancellation of dozens of flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico's army seizes local police weapons in cartel heartland amid gunfights, violence
item.description: The Mexican army has seized the firearms of local police in the Sinaloa city of Culiacan as cartel violence has escalated, dozens have been killed in the streets and distrust in the police grows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel under attack: 2 gunmen kill 6 civilians, injure others near Tel Aviv
item.description: A mass shooting by at least two individuals near Tel Aviv has killed and injured multiple people — an incident authorities believe is terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah confirms its leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in an Israeli airstrike
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s Hezbollah group confirmed on Saturday that its leader and one of its founding members, Hassan Nasrallah, was killed in an Israeli airstrike in a southern suburb of Beirut.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
